explanation:
## How to Calculate Your UBA XXI Final Grade

Your final grade combines two components: coursework (40%) and your final exam (60%). This calculator applies UBA's official formula instantly.

## The Formula

Final Grade = (0.4 × Coursework Average) + (0.6 × Final Exam Grade)

## Grading Notes

You must score at least 4.0 to pass. Coursework assignments are mandatory, but attendance is not. The final exam is required and must be taken in person.

## Important Disclaimer

This calculator is for reference only. For official grade verification and any critical academic decisions, consult the UBA XXI student portal or contact program advisors. Formula current as of 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.a: UBA XXI is Argentina's distance learning program offered by the University of Buenos Aires. It combines online and in-person components.

faq.q: What is the UBA XXI grading formula?
faq.a: Final Grade = (0.4 × Coursework Average) + (0.6 × Final Exam Grade). Coursework counts 40%, the final exam counts 60%.

faq.q: What is the minimum passing grade?
faq.a: You must score at least 4.0 out of 10 to pass a course at UBA XXI.

faq.q: Is attendance required at UBA XXI?
faq.a: No, attendance is not mandatory. However, completing all coursework assignments is required.

faq.q: Can I retake the final exam?
faq.a: Yes, you can retake exams as many times as needed until you achieve your desired grade.

faq.q: Is the final exam in-person or online?
faq.a: The final exam is mandatory and must be taken in person at an authorized UBA testing center.

faq.q: Do I need to pass to transfer to UBA's main campus?
faq.a: Yes, completing and passing UBA XXI courses is required to transition to UBA's in-person programs.

faq.q: Is this calculator free to use?
faq.a: Yes, this calculator and all our tools are completely free and require no registration.
